title: Tanacetum kittaryanum
intro: Tanacetum kittaryanum, also known as Kittaryan's tansy, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae), characterized by its yellow or white flowers and distinctive bitter taste. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum kittaryanum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Kittaryan's tansy include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Tanacetum kittaryanum is classified under the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a genus of flowering plants that belongs to the order Asterales. The species Tanacetum kittaryanum is native to Armenia and is a perennial herb. Tanacetum kittaryanum is further classified into the tribe Anthemideae, which comprises many other daisy-like plants. This classification indicates the plant's relationships with other flowering plants in its family and order.
uses: Tanacetum kittaryanum has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems. Its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in treating wounds, boils, and other skin infections. In some regions, the plant is also used to treat digestive issues, such as nausea and indigestion. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for these purposes. Its traditional medicinal uses are primarily based on anecdotal evidence and require further scientific study.

constituents: Tanacetum kittaryanum contains a range of active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and other secondary metabolites. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal and pharmacological properties. The sesquiterpene lactones, in particular, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Research has also identified the presence of phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, in Tanacetum kittaryanum. Further investigation into the plant's chemical composition may reveal additional active constituents and their potential uses.
preparations: Tanacetum kittaryanum is a plant species that is used in traditional medicine, and various herbal preparations have been developed from its extracts. The plant's leaves and flowers are often used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes. These herbal preparations are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can be beneficial for treating various health conditions.
